ANTLER.description: The pAntler launch block. Its Run lines start the MOOS apps in a mission community.

apptick.name: AppTick
apptick.description: Sets the target Iterate() rate for the ANTLER process, in Hertz. This controls pAntler itself, not the apps launched by Run lines.
apptick.basis: generated from bundled MOOS source in the local MOOS-IvP checkout

commstick.name: CommsTick
commstick.description: Sets the target MOOS communications processing rate for the ANTLER process, in Hertz. This controls pAntler's own mail handling.
commstick.basis: generated from bundled MOOS source in the local MOOS-IvP checkout

executablepath.name: ExecutablePath
executablepath.description: Sets the default directory used to locate executables launched by ANTLER. SYSTEMPATH means launched processes are resolved from the system path.
executablepath.basis: generated from bundled MOOS source in the local MOOS-IvP checkout

gentlekill.name: GentleKill
gentlekill.description: Controls whether ANTLER attempts a gentle shutdown of launched processes before stronger process termination.
gentlekill.basis: generated from bundled MOOS source in the local MOOS-IvP checkout
gentlekill.default: true on non-Windows, false on Windows

msbetweenlaunches.name: MSBetweenLaunches
msbetweenlaunches.description: Sets the delay, in milliseconds, between successive processes launched from the ANTLER block.
msbetweenlaunches.basis: generated from bundled MOOS source in the local MOOS-IvP checkout

newconsole.name: NewConsole
newconsole.description: Run option that starts the launched process in a separate console when true. It is normally written after the Run entry's @ options.
newconsole.basis: generated from bundled MOOS source in the local MOOS-IvP checkout

run.name: Run
run.description: Adds one process for ANTLER to launch. The value starts with the executable name and may include @ launch options and an optional ~ MOOS name.
run.basis: generated from bundled MOOS source in the local MOOS-IvP checkout

iBlinkStick.description: The iBlinkStick app works with the BlinkStick LED device.  The color and state of the LED can be controlled by posting a message, parsed by iBlinkStick.

qblink.name: qblink
qblink.description: Names the MOOS variable iBlinkStick subscribes to for LED blink/color commands. Incoming mail on this variable is translated into qblink.sh commands for the BlinkStick device.
qblink.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

iSay.description: The iSay application is a way to invoke the native speech generation utilities of OSX (the say command) and GNU/Linux (the espeak command).  It may also invoke the afplay commands available in both OSX and GNU/Linux to play a given wav or mp3 file.

app_logging.name: app_logging
app_logging.description: Enables AppCastingMOOSApp logging for iSay. This records app-level diagnostics and is separate from the speech/audio output itself.
app_logging.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

max_appcast_events.name: max_appcast_events
max_appcast_events.description: Limits how many appcast events iSay retains in its appcast report history. This affects diagnostic reporting, not which utterances are spoken.
max_appcast_events.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

volume.name: volume
volume.description: Sets the default playback volume for utterances and audio files. Named values include mute, vsoft, soft, normal, loud, and vloud; numeric values are clipped to the supported range.
volume.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

pContactMgrV20.description: The pContactMgrV20 app manages contact alerts, contact filtering, range reports, and contact visualization settings.

app_logging.name: app_logging
app_logging.description: Enables AppCastingMOOSApp logging for pContactMgrV20. This records app-level diagnostics and is separate from contact alert, range, and recap postings.
app_logging.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

eval_range_far.name: eval_range_far
eval_range_far.description: Sets the far threshold used when evaluating closest approach of contacts. Contacts whose closest range falls inside this threshold are counted as near encounters in summary metrics.
eval_range_far.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

eval_range_near.name: eval_range_near
eval_range_near.description: Sets the near threshold used when evaluating closest approach of contacts. Contacts whose closest range falls inside this tighter threshold are counted as very near encounters.
eval_range_near.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

hold_alerts_for_helm.name: hold_alerts_for_helm
hold_alerts_for_helm.description: Holds or delays contact-alert postings until pHelmIvP is ready to receive them. This prevents contact alerts from being sent before the helm is active.
hold_alerts_for_helm.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

max_appcast_events.name: max_appcast_events
max_appcast_events.description: Limits how many appcast events pContactMgrV20 retains for diagnostic reporting. This affects appcast history, not contact alert decisions.

max_contacts.name: max_contacts

max_retired_history.name: max_retired_history
max_retired_history.description: Limits how many retired contacts remain in the CONTACTS_RETIRED history. Values are clamped to the supported history range, and retired contacts are still reported at least once.
max_retired_history.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

ownship_group.name: ownship_group

ownship_type.name: ownship_type

range_report_timeout.name: range_report_timeout
range_report_timeout.description: Sets how long range-report data remains fresh before it is removed from range reports. This prevents stale contact ranges from lingering in posted summaries.
range_report_timeout.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

pHelmIvP.description: Runs the IvP Helm. It loads behavior files and publishes desired course, speed, and other decisions.

allow_park.name: allow_park
allow_park.description: Allows pHelmIvP to enter PARK mode when override/all-stop handling requests it. If false, the helm may stay in DRIVE even when a parking-style override is active.
allow_park.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

app_logging.name: app_logging
app_logging.description: Enables AppCastingMOOSApp logging for pHelmIvP. The value may be true or a logging target/file depending on the appcasting logging support.
app_logging.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

bhv_dir_not_found_ok.name: bhv_dir_not_found_ok
bhv_dir_not_found_ok.description: Allows startup to continue when an ivp_behavior_dir entry cannot be found. This is useful when mission files are shared across machines where optional behavior-library paths may not exist.
bhv_dir_not_found_ok.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

contact_max_age.name: contact_max_age
contact_max_age.description: Sets how long contact information remains usable before pHelmIvP treats the contact as stale and removes related contact behavior state. Values are accepted in the range 10 to 1800 seconds.
contact_max_age.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

goals_mandatory.name: goals_mandatory
goals_mandatory.description: Requires at least one non-constraint active goal behavior before the helm will produce normal decisions. When true, pHelmIvP warns or withholds action if only constraints are active.
goals_mandatory.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

helm_prefix.name: helm_prefix
helm_prefix.description: Sets a prefix added to selected helm status or decision variables posted by pHelmIvP. Use this when a mission needs helm output variables to be namespaced.
helm_prefix.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

hold_on_app.name: hold_on_app
hold_on_app.description: Names one MOOS app that must appear in DB_CLIENTS before pHelmIvP considers its startup hold satisfied. This delays helm-start messages until required support apps are connected.
hold_on_app.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

hold_on_apps.name: hold_on_apps
hold_on_apps.description: Names a comma-separated list of MOOS apps that must appear in DB_CLIENTS before pHelmIvP releases its startup hold. Each app name must be non-empty, unique, and contain no whitespace.
hold_on_apps.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

ivp_behavior_dir.name: ivp_behavior_dir
ivp_behavior_dir.description: Adds a directory searched for IvP behavior libraries referenced by the helm behavior file. Multiple ivp_behavior_dir lines may be used to add multiple search paths.
ivp_behavior_dir.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

kcache.name: kcache
kcache.description: Controls behavior-set caching in pHelmIvP. This is an advanced/debug setting for how the helm caches behavior-related information rather than an ordinary mission tuning knob.
kcache.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

max_appcast_events.name: max_appcast_events
max_appcast_events.description: Limits how many appcast events pHelmIvP retains for reporting. This affects diagnostic history in appcast output, not helm decision logic.
max_appcast_events.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

nav_grace.name: nav_grace
nav_grace.description: Sets how many seconds old ownship navigation data may be before pHelmIvP treats it as stale. If NAV_* data exceeds this age, the helm can warn or withhold decisions that require current navigation.
nav_grace.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

node_skew.name: node_skew

node_skew.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

ok_skew.name: ok_skew

ok_skew.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

other_override_var.name: other_override_var

other_override_var.basis: generated from local MOOS-IvP ivp/src source inventory

park_on_allstop.name: park_on_allstop

start_engaged.name: start_engaged
